rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and features.
Use implementation annexes for bindings and assignments that are valid only at the implementation level.
generic_rules: Ensure all AGREE identifiers and types are declared or visible in the AADL model.
Do not introduce package-qualified names or types not present in the input AADL model.
Place input range assumptions and output guarantees in component type contracts when bounds are explicitly stated.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `const` for named limits and `eq` for boolean expressions in contracts.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid in that scope.
Do not assume numeric bounds for ports unless explicitly stated in the requirement.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of component structure
